words: NELLIE WOOD SMITH: Yes, he would when they that new law and voted
in that pension business, that was at a time when he had already been working

speaker: NS

speaker: NS
words: Well, I don't-

speaker: NS
words: Oh, 53. He didn't retire until he was 83 years old. Because they

speaker: NS
words: couldn't find anybody else that could take the place. He ran every machine

speaker: NS
words: in the machine shop. He would sometimes have two or three of them going at time. He'd

words: those things. They maybe could find somebody that could run one of them but to

words: have somebody to take care of all of them, they'd have to hire five or six men, or

speaker: NS
words: three or four, you know, to take care of all those things. Some of them

speaker: NS

speaker: NS

speaker: NS
words: He started firing the engine and cleaning up around them. And he just grew up

speaker: NS

words: there or setting things up in their lathe - why, he was watching him and he got

speaker: NS
words: so he could do it. He just learned from his older brother. Just all he needed

speaker: NS
words: to know. He's quite efficient at it in no time. And then after he was kinda

speaker: NS
words: grown-up the owner of the shop put him on in to the foundary. They had a foundary

speaker: NS
words: in connection with their machine shop and he used to be molder in that foundary

speaker: NS
words: and would mold out big things and all.

speaker: SS

speaker: NS

speaker: NS
words: Well, there was another man that had been a great friend of thei rs, Mr. Hollenbeck.

speaker: NS
words: And he had come West and had wrote back to them and said, "Why don't you

speaker: NS
words: guys all come out here?" He says, "Everything's kinda new and original, you know,

speaker: NS
words: and everything's kinda wide open and lots of things to do, you know. And lots of

speaker: NS
words: jobs. There's a possibility, you know, of lots of work, you know." And he said,

speaker: NS
words: "Why don't you come on out here?" And he liked tt so well and they liked the

words: climate and liked everything about it. And they had been here quite a while. And so,

speaker: NS
words: I guess,

speaker: NS

speaker: NS
words: they liked it. And peetty soon they had hired out cutting cordwood. And they

words: found that job - a man wanted some cordwood cut. So they hired out to him.

words: Tbey thought that would do for a meal ticket until they found something different.

words: And

speaker: NS

words: good

speaker: NS

speaker: NS

words: wilted lettuce. And we had our own baked bread, homemade bread, you know. And, I

words: don't know what all.

words: else's list, you know, - a whole wagon load. And then you'd go back over to

words: his place - that was about a mile to where they lived. And there's where he'd

words: leave the stuff - Millers . And we'd^et ours and take it on home. And we'd have

words: know

speaker: NS

words: the folks - well, we got a kick out of them. During the daytime the bunch of us

words: the

speaker: NS

speaker: NS

speaker: NS
words: school. Well, then when we moved in town - I don't know if that made any

words: difference in it or not but anyhow it wasn't long until the group moved into Bovill,

speaker: NS
words: that is part of 'em, anyway. And I think things kinda closed down at the mine or

speaker: NS
words: something. But anyhow they came in and the Henry Smiths was living here at

speaker: NS
words: Helmer and then Ernie was out there. And he started into - to move in or

speaker: NS
words: to come into Bovill and, of course, he knew we were living here. We moved away

words: out there. He dropped in to see us and all and got a job right now. They put him busy

words: upside down. And so - I don't know whether he went up to the shops to see Henry

words: in

speaker: NS

speaker: NS
words: one of the engine's, I think, in that book. But, they run their cable line way

speaker: NS
words: in

speaker: NS
words: and then its loaded onto the car, you know, and that's the way that the logging

speaker: NS

speaker: NS

words: from his working in the East. So . he hired.over to the shops and started working

speaker: NS
words: there. He went from one job to another, whatever was ground/and finally he

speaker: NS

speaker: NS
words: bookwork that maybe he couldn't handle mostly. And the responsibility of it. He

speaker: NS
words: didn't like that responsibility but he liked to do the work. Then it got so that after

words: he was put in charge of the lathes^hy someone from camp'd telephoned in that,

words: well. "Enaine number so and so has got to have a new brass. Bring out a general."

words: Or something like that. And they could tell him engine number so and so - he knew

speaker: NS

speaker: NS
words: know,

speaker: NS

speaker: NS
words: Or

speaker: NS
